intro: Hoplophytum nudicaule, also known as Hoplophytum, is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hoplophytum nudicaule include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hoplophytum nudicaule is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and rheumatism.
